records.ety: [Cf. OF. plaissier to bend, and also F. plisser to plait, L. plicare, plicitum, to fold, lay, or wind together. Cf. Plash to pleach.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Pleached (plēcht); p. pr. & vb. n. Pleaching.]

defs: To unite by interweaving, as branches of trees; to plash; to interlock.
